name: Thai Ginger Candy
recipeIngredient: Young ginger (14 oz), peeled and sliced into 1/8 inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the ginger slices in a pot and cover with enough water to submerge all the pieces. Bring to a gentle boil, then let it continue boiling for several minutes before draining. Repeat this process 3-4 times to remove excess spiciness.
2. Poke holes in the ginger slices using a fork or bamboo skewer, and blot them dry with paper towels. Place the ginger and syrup mixture in a large glass jar.
3. In a pot, combine 1 1/2 cups sugar, 2 cups water, 2 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp lime juice. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then remove from heat and allow it to cool. Pour the syrup into the jar and cover tightly. Let stand overnight.
4. The next day, pour out the syrup from the jar into another pot. Add 2 cups of sugar and 2 tsp salt, bringing the mixture to a boil until dissolved. Allow it to cool before returning the syrup to the jar. Cover and let stand overnight.
5. On the following day, repeat step 4 but with only half a cup of sugar. Let stand overnight again.
6. Repeat step 5 once more, this time using just 1/2 cup sugar, and allow it to stand for an additional two nights.
7. Strain out the syrup, leaving only the ginger pieces behind. Place the ginger in a single layer on a foil-lined cookie sheet and dry in direct sunlight or at the lowest oven temperature until glazed and dried. Store any remaining sugar (measured at the beginning of the recipe) over the ginger pieces in an airtight container. Keeps up to 4 months. Serve with unsweetened tea or other hot beverages.
